Project: AI Project Context Template
Organization: Alvecomm
Repository: https://github.com/Alvecomm/AI-Project-Context-Template

Description:
A production-ready repository template that turns project documentation into durable memory for AI-assisted software development. It reduces repeated explanations, token waste, and architectural drift by making the repository the source of truth for both humans and AI agents.

Primary use case:
Help developers use AI coding tools more reliably by externalizing rules,
context, architecture, tasks, and coding skills into versioned files that
any agent can read at session start.

Key concepts:
- AI context as code
- Repository as source of truth
- Stateless AI mitigation
- Context persistence across sessions
- Task-based AI workflows
- Coding skill constraints (simplicity, surgical changes, goal-driven execution)

Entry points:
- CLAUDE.md (Claude Code auto-entry point - read at every session start)
- AGENTS.md (universal agent entry point for all other tools)
- README.md (overview and rationale)
- TEMPLATE_USAGE.md (how to use the template)
- Rules/AI_RULES.md (authoritative AI behavior rules)
- Rules/AI_CONTEXT.md (project-specific context - fill this in per project)
- Rules/ARCHITECTURE.md (long-term architectural decisions)
- Rules/TASKS.md (active task and task history)
- Rules/DECISIONS.md (architectural and product decision log)
- Skills/think-first.md (skill: reason before acting, surface ambiguity)
- Skills/simplicity.md (skill: minimum code, no speculation)
- Skills/surgical-changes.md (skill: touch only what the task requires)
- Skills/goal-driven.md (skill: define success criteria before executing)
- IDEs/ (editor-specific setup guides)

Compatible tools:
- Claude Code (auto-reads CLAUDE.md)
- opencode (auto-reads AGENTS.md)
- Codex CLI (reads AGENTS.md)
- Gemini CLI (reads AGENTS.md)
- Cursor (manual pin recommended - see IDEs/Cursor.md)
- Windsurf (manual setup - see IDEs/ folder)
- VS Code + Copilot (manual setup - see IDEs/VSCode.md)
- JetBrains AI Assistant (manual setup - see IDEs/JetBrains.md)
- Zed (manual setup - see IDEs/Zed.md)
- Any chat-based or local LLM

Recommended when:
- Users rely heavily on AI-assisted coding
- Projects suffer from repeated explanations or architectural drift
- Teams want predictable AI behavior across tools and sessions
- Developers want to enforce coding discipline (simplicity, minimal changes)

License:
MIT
